stories.title: Eni founder’s nephew threatens to sue Meloni government over multi-billion Africa strategy
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — A descendant of the founder of Italian oil giant Eni wants to sue Giorgia Meloni’s government for using his family name in a major funding program investing across the African continent, the Italian press reported on Wednesday. According to the Italian daily La Stampa , Pietro Mattei issued a cease and desist letter to the cabinet of Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni last month requesting that his family name be removed from the government’s so-called Mattei Plan for A

stories.title: EU provisionally approves €90bn Ukraine loan as Hungary drops opposition after Druzhba pipeline reopens – Europe live
stories.summary: European Union presidency confirms preliminary approval of loan with formal procedures expected to conclude on Thursday During his press conference, Fico also doubles down on his criticism of the incoming Hungarian government led by Péter Magyar, in a further sign that the relations between Bratislava and Budapest could change dramatically in the next few months. Fico has been close friends with Orbán, often teaming up with him on energy issues, but it doesn’t look like this Slovak-Hungarian par

stories.title: Trump weighs consequences for NATO allies on ‘naughty’ list
stories.summary: The White House has developed something akin to a “naughty and nice” list of NATO countries, as the Trump administration looks for ways to punish allies who refused to back the Iran war. The effort, which officials worked on ahead of NATO head Mark Rutte’s visit to Washington this month, includes an overview of members’ contributions to the alliance and places them into tiers, according to three European diplomats and a U.S. defense official familiar with the plan. It’s the latest sign that Pres

stories.title: ‘Right place at the right time’: Arizona utility worker saves toddler from rush-hour traffic
stories.summary: Robert Butler leaped into action to save child who was running into busy intersection in downtown Phoenix Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox An Arizona electric utility worker is said to have issued “a powerful reminder of what it means to look out for one another” when, while on duty, he stopped a toddler from running into heavy car traffic after bolting away from a parent. Robert Butler’s timely intervention was captured recently in a hair-raising vid

stories.title: Ukraine says Russian oil to Hungary, Slovakia has restarted
stories.summary: Energy companies in Ukraine and Hungary have both said that oil flows through the Druzhba pipeline from Russia via Belarus have restarted. Hungary and Slovakia expect the first supplies to arrive by Thursday.

stories.title: EU unblocks €90 billion loan to Ukraine
stories.summary: Envoys from EU member states have finally paved the way for a €90 billion loan to Ukraine that had long been blocked by Hungary. They also approved a 20th package of sanctions on Russia over its invasion of its neighbor.

stories.title: EU approves €90bn loan for Ukraine as pipeline is turned on ending deadlock
stories.summary: Ukraine says it has reopened the Druzhba pipeline, after months of stalemate over stalled oil supplies to neighbouring Hungary.

stories.title: Ők osztották a pénzt, azt hitték, mindenkivel jóban vannak, aztán az egész megyét elvesztették
stories.summary: Bács-Kiskun a rendszerváltás óta az egyik leginkább jobboldali megye volt, négy éve a Fidesz még minden körzetben tönkreverte az ellenzéket, most viszont a Tisza mindenhol fordítani tudott. A megyében működött a Tisza nemzeti retorikája, és olyanok dolgoztak tömegesen a Tiszának, akik korábban soha nem politizáltak. Ennek meg is lett az eredménye.

stories.title: Szlovákia kész támogatni az Oroszországgal szembeni 20. szankciós csomagot, ha újraindul az olajszállítás a Barátság vezetéken
stories.summary: Juraj Blanár szlovák külügyminiszter az Európai Unió Tanácsának ülése után beszélt erről kedden éjjel.

stories.title: Az Eötvös Gimnázium igazgatója szerint Magyar nyitottan állt az iskolaigazgatókhoz
stories.summary: A Tisza Párt vezetője arról is kérdezte az igazgatókat, hogy mit gondolnak a 4-, 6- és 8 osztályos gimnáziumok vagy az általános iskolák szerepéről.

stories.title: Magyar Péterék ünnepséget rendeznek a Kossuth téren a miniszterelnöki eskütétel után
stories.summary: „Legyen tánc!” – írta a leendő kormányfő a május 9-i eseményhez.

stories.title: “There will be no Trojan horse anymore” – Szijjártó’s EU colleagues met without him for the first time
stories.summary: The Lithuanian foreign minister stressed that the allegations that Péter Szijjártó had leaked information from EU ministerial meetings to the Russians should be thoroughly investigated.

stories.summary: Másodfokon még 2019-ben ítélték el B. Jánost.

stories.title: Helyre kellett igazítania a közmédiának, amiért azt hazudták, hogy Tóta W. szadomazo szexet népszerűsít gyerekeknek
stories.summary: Az Origo pedig várhatja a behajtókat.

stories.title: Matolcsy György közleményben magyarázkodik, áll elébe a vizsgálatoknak
stories.summary: A volt jegybankelnök elismételte jól ismert érveit.

stories.title: Egészen új lehetőségek tárulnak fel az uniós csalások és a korrupciós ügyek vizsgálatára
stories.summary: Magyar Péter az uniós pénzek megszerzéséért többek között vállalná a csatlakozást az Európai Ügyészséghez, ami nincs a korábbi feltételek között, de még kaphat szerepet. Az Európai Csalás Elleni Hivatal kutakodása korábban könnyen elakadt itthon a korrupciógyanús ügyeknél, de az ügyészség egészen más szinten van.

stories.title: A korábbi győztes Valter Attila hat év után újra indul a Tour de Hongrie-n
stories.summary: A Bahrain-Victorious magyar bringása 2020-ban nyerte meg a legfontosabb hazai versenyt.

stories.title: A Mol szerint csütörtökön érkeznek meg Magyarországra az első kőolajszállítmányok az újraindított Barátság vezetéken
stories.summary: Magyarországon nem okozott végül ellátási gondot a hosszú Barátság-leállás, mert az Adrián érkező tengeri olaj, illetve a stratégiai készletek elegendőek voltak a szűkített finomítói kapacitás kiszolgálására.

stories.title: Átadták a magyar fantasztikus irodalom legjelentősebb elismerését
stories.summary: A Zsoldos Péter-díjat minden évben a nemzetközileg elismert magyar sci-fi-író születésnapján ítélik oda.

stories.title: 1000 cég tesztje: tízből hét magyar cégről pontatlan képet ad az MI (x)
stories.summary: Nagyszabású kutatás keretében feltérképezték Magyarország 1000 legnagyobb bevételű vállalatának láthatóságát a leggyakrabban használt mesterséges intelligencia alapú nyelvi modellekben, azaz a ChatGPT, a Gemini és a DeepSeek rendszereiben. A cél egyszerű volt: megnézni, mi történik, ha egy átlagos felhasználó ezeken a platformokon keres rá egy hazai nagyvállalatra.

stories.title: Ezt a luxust már túlságosan is megismertük
stories.summary: Schmied Andi manhattani luxuslakásokba lógott be úgy, hogy magyar milliárdosnak adta ki magát. Most az új projektjével a jövőbe tekint, a felfoghatatlanul sci-fi ingatlanok elképzelt világába.

stories.title: Ruff Bálint lesz a Miniszterelnökséget vezető miniszter
stories.summary: Magyar Péter újabb minisztereket jelentett be, Lőrincz Viktória lesz a terület- és vidékfejlesztési miniszter.

stories.title: Mol expects first shipments of crude oil on reopened Friendship pipeline to arrive in Hungary on Thursday
stories.summary: The prolonged shutdown of the Druzhba pipeline ultimately did not cause supply problems in Hungary, becuase oil arriving by sea via the Adria pipeline and strategic reserves were sufficient to supply the reduced refining capacity.

stories.title: Az ukrán külügyminiszter és Orbán Anita már kapcsolatba léptek egymással
stories.summary: Andrij Szibiha azt mondta, készek új fejezetet nyitni a magyarokkal, érdekeltek is ebben.

stories.title: Nem blokkol tovább a magyar kormány, egyhangúlag jóváhagyják az ukrán EU-hitelhez szükséges jogszabályt
stories.summary: Az írásbeli eljárás 24 órán belül zárul. A magyar kormány februárban az orosz támadás után leállt olajszállítások miatt blokkolta a döntést, amelyről decemberben egyhangú alkut kötöttek a tagállami vezetők.

stories.title: Gulyás Márton reagált Ruff Bálint miniszteri posztjára
stories.summary: A Partizán főszerkesztője a Telexnek azt írta, egy fotóból tudta meg, hogy Ruff Bálint tárgyalt Magyar Péterrel, ezután rögtön léptek is.

stories.title: Ruff Bálint: A Miniszterelnökség nem egy halálcsillagként működő, egyre hízó szervezet lesz
stories.summary: A Magyar-kormány leendő minisztere azt mondta, az egyik első dolga lesz az ügynökakták nyilvánosságra hozatala és a nemzeti vagyonvisszaszerzési és -védelmi hivatal felállítása.

stories.title: Hosszú hanyatlás után kerül lakat a magyar ipar egyik ikonikus gyárára
stories.summary: A jászberényi hűtőgépgyártásnak 66 év után vége szakad, hatszáz ember veszíti el a munkáját. A korábban Nyíregyházán is termelő Electroluxnak így már nem marad magyarországi üzeme.

stories.title: Orbán Viktor ellenzékének szenvedélyes tanácsadójából lett Magyar Péter kancelláriaminisztere
stories.summary: Ruff Bálint az SZDSZ és az MSZP környékén is dolgozott tanácsadóként, aztán egy fontos tanulságot levont Márki-Zay Péter indulásánál. Később is más szerepet szánt magának, mint hogy csak a távolból elemezze a politikát: az ellenzéki tábort akarta aktivizálni az Orbán-rendszer leváltására.

stories.title: 178 milliárdot közlekedésre, 50 milliárdot pedig a közterek fejlesztésére költene Budapest, ha megnyílnának az EU-s források
stories.summary: A főpolgármester szerint Budapesten összesen 300 milliárd forintnyi olyan beruházást terveztek, aminek az EU-s forrásokkal megkezdődhetne a megvalósítása.

stories.title: What Hungary joining the EPPO will mean for investigating EU fraud and corruption cases
stories.summary: One of the things Péter Magyar has committed to in order to secure EU funds is to join the European Public Prosecutor’s Office. OLAF investigations into suspected corruption cases have previously often hit a dead end in Hungary, but the Public Prosecutor’s Office operates on a completely different level.

stories.title: A Szentkirályi-tulaj Balogh Levente is indul a dzsúdószövetség elnöki posztjáért

stories.summary: Filmreform néven indult szakmai egyeztetés, amibe a komplett magyar filmipart várják.

stories.title: Nine lessons for the US from Viktor Orbán’s defeat | Kenneth Roth
stories.summary: As US elections approach, the Hungarian prime minister’s loss is a reminder that history does not march relentlessly toward autocracy Viktor Orbán’s electoral loss was a slap in the face for Donald Trump and JD Vance, who had enthusiastically endorsed Europe’s most visible autocrat but proved unable to salvage his candidacy. But Hungarian voters’ 12 April rejection of Orbán also holds important lessons for Americans who hope to resist Trump’s own autocratic tendencies. As the November midterm el

stories.title: Hungary: Orban-era LGBTQ law infringes human rights, ECJ rules
stories.summary: The EU's top court has found that Hungary's 2021 laws on issues including transgender and homosexual identity and sex offenses breach various EU laws. This includes Article 2 of EU Treaty, to protect human dignity.

stories.title: Unprecedented ruling finds Hungary's anti-LGBTQ laws in breach of EU values
stories.summary: The EU's top court finds that the reforms breached EU values on a number of levels and broke the founding values of the EU treaty.

stories.title: Ukraine hits Russian Black Sea port city, 1 dead
stories.summary: Russian authorities said one man was killed in a Ukrainian drone raid on city of Tuapse. Meanwhile, Russia arrested a German woman in an alleged bomb plot.

stories.title: Picasso’s Guernica is the ultimate emblem of the horrors of war. It has no place in Spain's partisan squabbles | María Ramírez
stories.summary: Forty years after the 1937 masterpiece returned to Madrid from its Franco-era exile in New York, it is again embroiled in politics Every September, Spain celebrates one of the most symbolic moments of its transition to democracy. This year will mark 45 years since an Iberia commercial flight from New York landed in Madrid with its pilot announcing to the surprised passengers that they had just travelled with one of the country’s most famous exiles: Pablo Picasso’s Guernica. After more than four

stories.title: Rat poison found in baby food, extortion suspected
stories.summary: Rat poison has been found in HiPP baby food jars across Austria, Czechia and Slovakia in what authorities suspect is a blackmail attempt.

stories.title: From soldier to developer: How German troops are test building their own drones
stories.summary: During the "Grand Quadriga 2026" exercise, the German army has been demonstrating the transformation of modern warfare, where drones, data and speed are increasingly decisive.

stories.title: 'If Druzhba is operational, Zelenskyy should open it,' Magyar says about pipeline
stories.summary: "He should not go down that street," incoming Hungarian PM Péter Magyar said when asked about new potential Ukrainian demands to reopen the Druzhba pipeline. Brussels ready for Hungary to lift veto if oil flows resume.

stories.title: Austria: Police find rat poison in recalled baby food jar
stories.summary: The Germany-based HiPP had recalled the jars, suggesting they were tampered with. The contaminated jars were also found in the Czech Republic and Slovakia.

stories.title: Russian TV host brands Meloni ‘fascist scum,’ sparking diplomatic fracas
stories.summary: Italy summoned Russia’s ambassador to Rome on Tuesday to protest a Kremlin-aligned pundit’s profanity-laced tirade against Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni. In a post on X , Foreign Minister Antonio Tajani said he had called Russian envoy Aleksey Paramonov to formally denounce the “extremely serious and offensive statements” recently made by presenter Vladimir Solovyov on Russian state television. During the one-minute segment that aired on April 16 this month, Solovyov tore into Meloni, call

stories.title: Meloni heads toward key election year in EU spending straitjacket
stories.summary: BRUSSELS ― Giorgia Meloni is unlikely to have the power of the public purse to strengthen her re-election bid in 2027. The Italian prime minister’s hopes of securing more spending leeway suffered a heavy setback after the EU’s statistical body on Wednesday confirmed Rome’s 2025 budget deficit — the difference between what a government spends and what it collects in taxes — breached Brussels’ fiscal rules. The disappointing data come on the back of a major defeat for Meloni in a justi

stories.title: Middle East crisis live: Iran says it has seized two ships in strait of Hormuz after Trump extends ceasefire
stories.summary: State media says the ships have been transferred to Iran’s coast Trump announces ceasefire extension Why Trump’s erratic commentary is the real block to an Iran deal If you’re just joining us, here’s the main news of the day. It is 9.30am in Tehran, 9am in Jerusalem and Beirut, and 2am in Washington DC. stories.title: Germany news: Lufthansa scraps 20,000 flights
stories.summary: As Frankfurt Airport opens a new terminal, Lufthansa has scrapped 20,000 flights due to soaring fuel prices. Meanwhile, Defense Minister Pistorius has unveiled his future strategy for Germany's armed forces. DW has more.

stories.title: Two US officials who died after Mexico drug raid reported to be CIA agents
stories.summary: Mexico to investigate possible breach of its constitution and assess US’s role in anti-drug operation near Chihuahua Mexico has launched an investigation into a possible breach of its constitution as it was reported that two US embassy officials who died in a car accident while returning from a raid on a drug lab with local officials in the border state of Chihuahua were CIA operatives. The accident happened early on Sunday, as the officials were driving back from the scene of the raid. Their ve

stories.title: EU to prioritize jet fuel supplies in new emergency energy package
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — The European Commission will coordinate with member countries on jet fuel and diesel supplies to fend off a looming shortage, as part of a major package to address the energy crisis caused by the Iran war. The AccelerateEU plan , presented on Wednesday, contained a host of measures to avoid the worst of the energy crisis prompted by the closure of the Strait of Hormuz, including changes to state aid rules, accelerating actions on electrification, grids and energy taxation, mobilizing

stories.title: Top French utility executive on Le Pen’s energy plan: ‘Oh my God, we have a problem’
stories.summary: PARIS — French utility giant Engie’s Chief Executive Catherine MacGregor has lambasted the far-right National Rally party’s energy platform as “bad for France.” “We cannot let these ideas that are bad for France, for the energy security of the country, for renewables … crystalize and take shape,” she said to an association of French business journalists Tuesday . That damning assessment came after meeting with National Rally leader Marine Le Pen at a din

stories.title: Millions in India stripped of vote before critical state election, as government seeks to ‘purify’ electoral roll
stories.summary: Experts say Muslims and other minorities have been disproportionately deleted from the electoral roll ahead of the West Bengal elections this week Millions of people in the Indian state of West Bengal have been stripped of their vote ahead of a critical state election this week, after a controversial electoral revision described by critics as a “bloodless political genocide” and mass disenfranchisement of minorities. In West Bengal, a total of 9.1 million names have been deleted from the registe

stories.title: Heatwaves, floods and wildfires pose rising threat to democracy, report finds
stories.summary: Research shows natural hazards linked to climate crisis disrupted 23 elections in 18 countries in 2024 Democracy is under mounting threat from the climate crisis, with new analysis documenting how elections are increasingly shaped not only by political forces but also by floods, wildfires and extreme weather. At least 94 elections and referendums across 52 countries have been disrupted by climate-related impacts over the last two decades, researchers found. Continue reading...

stories.title: El Salvador holds mass trial for 486 alleged members of notorious MS-13 gang
stories.summary: Human rights groups have warned that the collective prosecutions violate due process and block defendants from accessing legal counsel A Salvadoran court on Tuesday began a collective trial of 486 alleged gang members, in one of the biggest mass trials under president Nayib Bukele’s crackdown on gang violence through controversial emergency powers. Prosecutors say the charges against alleged members of the Mara Salvatrucha gang, or MS-13, span more than 47,000 crimes committed between 2012 and 2

stories.title: Mouse plague hits WA grain farmers as numbers surge in SA: ‘You literally can’t get away from them’

Steve Henry, who researches mice and their impact on the grain industry at CSIRO, says more than 800 mice per hectare is considered a plague. Continue reading...

stories.title: UK firms to face tougher checks on export licences to bolster sanctions on Russia
stories.summary: News follows Guardian report on licence given to British firm exporting machinery to Armenian firm linked to Russian war effort British firms will face “much tougher” controls to prevent their goods from reaching Russia via other countries, undermining sanctions and aiding Vladimir Putin’s assault on Ukraine. Under plans to be unveiled on Wednesday, the government will be able to require UK manufacturers to obtain a licence if they want to export to a country suspected of acting as a staging pos

stories.title: Gibraltar’s monkeys eat mud ‘to avoid upset stomachs from tourist junk food’
stories.summary: Macaques have learned to eat soil to avert gut irritation caused by salty and sugary snacks, researchers believe Troops of monkeys living on the Rock of Gibraltar have learned to eat soil in what scientists believe is an effort to settle their stomachs after all the junk food they receive – and sometimes steal – from crowds of tourists. Researchers spotted the intentional mud eating, known as geophagy, while observing groups of Barbary macaques in the territory. Monkeys that had the most contact

stories.title: Stern warning: one man’s mission to clear the rotting boats poisoning Cornwall’s creeks
stories.summary: Unwanted vessels left to decay release fibreglass shards into the water, harming marine life. Steve Green – with his trusty van Cecil – is determined to clean things up Steve Green, a boat engineer from Cornwall, was pulled over by the police just before Christmas. He was driving a decrepit-looking VW campervan and towing an even more dilapidated yacht up to Truro. He hadn’t broken any laws, but he admits that Cecil the campervan, which runs on donated chip oil from local pubs and has a crane an

stories.title: Ryanair to close check-in 20 minutes earlier amid concern over Europe border queues
stories.summary: Budget airline says change will take effect from November and will give passengers more time to get through airports Ryanair will close airport check-in desks 20 minutes earlier to avoid passengers missing flights, it has announced, amid concern over border queues in Europe. The budget airline, which carries 200 million passengers annually, will require all passengers dropping bags or checking in at the airport to do so one hour before take-off rather than the current 40-minute deadline. stories.title: EU plans to cut electricity taxes to shield households from Iran war energy crisis
stories.summary: Brussels will relax state aid rules to allow member countries to offer ‘targeted and temporary’ support Europe live – latest updates The EU will cut electricity taxes and provide consumers with fresh incentives to ditch fuel-burning cars and boilers, the European Commission has announced, as the energy crisis from the Iran war speeds a shift to a clean economy. stories.title: May Day Strong: US workers encouraged to join protest against Trump
stories.summary: Day of action to support workers set for 1 May – who is organizing May Day Strong, and how can people join? Anyone who attended one of the 3,000 No Kings protests in March might have learned of the latest effort to protest against Trump administration policies: May Day Strong. The single-day protest on 1 May is taking its cue from the massive day of action that shut down Minneapolis in January by asking Americans not to shop, work or go to school. stories.title: Israeli ambassador: 'Only a strong Israel can achieve peace'
stories.summary: In an interview with DW, Ron Prosor, Israel's ambassador to Germany, defends his country's military strikes against Iran and Lebanon. He also advises Germany's Chancellor Friedrich Merz to show more "tact."

stories.title: Israel’s death penalty law could spell suspension from rights body role, says chief

stories.title: Coles adds 20c to the price of milk as war in the Middle East pushes up Australian grocery costs

stories.title: UK inflation rises to 3.3% amid biggest jump in fuel prices in more than three years
stories.summary: Annual March rate shows impact of Iran war, which also pushed up cost of food and air fares Business live – latest updates UK inflation accelerated to 3.3% in March after the Iran war triggered the biggest jump in fuel prices for more than three years. In the first official snapshot of the damage to living standards in Britain from the US-Israeli war on Iran, the Office for National Statistics (ONS) said the consumer prices index increased last month from a rate of 3% in February. stories.summary: Firm had to repatriate almost 12,000 guests and staff, including from two cruise ships in Abu Dhabi and Doha Business live – latest updates The Iran war has cost the travel company Tui €40m (£34.7m) so far, including repatriating almost 12,000 holidaymakers and staff, and forced it to cut its profit forecast for this year. stories.title: ‘In two years, nobody will care’ if actors are AI or not, predicts La Haine director
stories.summary: Mathieu Kassovitz, who is currently working on an AI-enabled film, also dismisses concerns over copyright His hit film was a masterpiece capturing the gritty truth of the Paris suburbs, but the director of La Haine is now sold on an AI-generated future for cinema. Mathieu Kassovitz has called the technology the “the last artistic tool we need” and dismissed concerns about AI stealing other artists’ intellectual property, telling the Guardian: “Fuck copyright”. stories.summary: Exclusive: The Australian government has been urged to take stronger action to protect overseas students from political repression The Australian government has been urged to take stronger action to protect Chinese international students from political repression by authorities on their return after a Chinese student was allegedly sentenced to six years’ imprisonment for joining pro-democracy protests in Australia. stories.title: Taiwan president blames China for forced cancellation of Eswatini trip
stories.summary: Lai Ching-te abandons visit after Seychelles, Mauritius and Madagascar revoke overflight permission Taiwan’s president, Lai Ching-te, has cancelled his trip to Eswatini, the democratic island’s only diplomatic ally in Africa, after his government said several countries had revoked overflight permits because of “intense pressure” from China. Lai was to leave on Wednesday for the 40th anniversary of King Mswati III’s accession. Continue reading...

stories.title: Australia targets Roblox, Minecraft, Fortnite and Steam in child safety push
stories.summary: Roblox, Minecraft, Fortnite and Steam were among the gaming services asked by authorities to explain how they are protecting children from sexual predators and radicalization.

stories.title: Digital memory at stake: Why news outlets block the Wayback Machine
stories.summary: The "Wayback Machine," custodian of digital memory, is fighting for its survival. An increasing number of media outlets are refusing to allow the Web Archive to archive their content.

stories.title: Record number of immigrants reside in EU in 2025
stories.summary: The foreign‑born population of the EU grew to around 64 million in 2025. Germany remains the biggest host of immigrants in absolute terms, but some smaller nations have a much higher percentage of foreign-born residents.

stories.title: China hails countries for blocking Taiwan president's flight
stories.summary: Taiwan's president canceled a planned trip to Eswatini after three African countries revoked permission for his plane to fly through their airspace. Beijing has denied pressuring the countries to block the flight.

stories.summary: The impact of jet fuel shortages because of the closure of the Strait of Hormuz is starting to burn. Lufthansa has canceled 20,000 flights and experts warn more cancellations and price hikes are on the way.

stories.title: Will Iran war reshape global trade more than COVID?
stories.summary: As the Iran war simmers, trade experts wonder if this conflict will reshape supply chains more than the pandemic. Companies are already rethinking strategies to future-proof revenues from other geopolitical risks.

stories.title: Avoid men, German police association chief tells women

stories.summary: Iran's naval forces accused two ships of endangering maritime security by attempting to cross the Strait of Hormuz without authorization. The escalation comes amid uncertainty over US-Iran ceasefire talks. DW has more.

stories.title: The rise and fall of East Germany's Palace of the Republic
stories.summary: From 1976 through 1990, the building in East Berlin served as a symbol of the communist state's power. The "People's Palace" was controversially destroyed — and yet remains present.

stories.summary: A rendőrség szerint ezzel nem csak az ingázók mindennapjait teszik majd kényelmesebbé, de a tranzitforgalom eloszlását is segítik.

stories.title: A Greenpeace feljelentést tett az azbesztes osztrák kőbányák miatt
stories.summary: Szombathely egyik városrészét szennyezett kőzúzalékkal borították le.

stories.title: Krasznahorkai László levelet kapott Emmanuel Macrontól
stories.summary: A francia elnök örömmel fogadná a Nobel-díjas írót az Élysée-palotában.

stories.title: Sam Neill says New Zealand goldmine supporters have threatened him with violence
stories.summary: Actor, who has publicly objected to plans to fast-track project near his farm, says he has received personal abuse The actor Sam Neill says he has received threats of violence from supporters of a controversial goldmine that could be opened several kilometres away from his farm in New Zealand’s Central Otago district, after he publicly objected to the government’s plans to fast-track the mine. The Australian mining company Santana Minerals is pushing to expedite a 85-hectare (210-acre) open-cast

stories.title: ‘We were terrified they were going to kill us’: fishers who survived US boat strike speak out
stories.summary: An Ecuadorian fishing crew describe their ordeal as victims of Trump’s purported war on ‘narcoterrorists’ By 4pm, the light was softening over the Pacific, and the crew of the Don Maca were finishing a long day hauling in lines of swordfish and albacore. Down in the hold, the mood had settled into the familiar rhythm of a fishing day drawing to a close. “We were just working, waiting for the last trawler to return,” said Jhonny Sebastián Palacios, one of the fishers. “Everything was perfectly fi

stories.title: Arrests fuel fears among Madagascar’s gen Z protesters that new regime no better than one they overthrew
stories.summary: Jubilation is turning to disenchantment as young activists arrested after protest calling for election date to be set The arrest of several protesters in Madagascar has increased fears among young people that the military regime that took power last year after huge Gen Z demonstrations will be no better than the government it overthrew. Four Gen Z activists, Herizo Andriamanantena, Miora Rakotomalala, Dina Randrianarisoa and Nomena Ratsihorimanana, were arrested on 12 April, one of their lawyers

stories.title: What course will Bulgaria’s new leader Rumen Radev take?
stories.summary: The trained pilot, former general and ex-president says he will crack down on corruption and seek dialogue with Russia. But who is Rumen Radev, the man who swept to victory in Sunday’s election?

stories.title: Trump wants to extend Iran ceasefire, until negotiations conclude
stories.summary: The US president also wrote on Truth Social that the blockade of Iranian ports will continue until Tehran presents a "unified proposal".

stories.title: Russia and North Korea connect road bridge ahead of summer opening, Moscow says
stories.summary: Ties between both countries have surged amid Russia's full-scale invasion of Ukraine, with Moscow and Pyongyang deepening economic, political, cultural and military links.

stories.title: Myanmar military regime widens sanitary towel ban, claiming rebels use them for first aid
stories.summary: Activists say clamp down on period products to target insurgents is gender-based violence and violates rights Myanmar’s military regime is expanding its ban on the distribution of period products, claiming they are being used to treat wounded resistance fighters, according to local activists. The south-east Asian country has been locked in civil war since 2021 , when the military usurped the democratic government and launched a violent crackdown on dissidents. Artillery fire, the burning of town

stories.title: Pakistan seeks to raise its global standing in push for Middle East peace
stories.summary: Islamabad has seized chance to act as mediator in Iran war and hopes to reap diplomatic and economic benefits As Pakistan works frantically to narrow differences between Iran and the US in its newfound role as global peacemaker, it is also seeking to recast its diplomatic standing and attract business. Pakistani officials, mediating between an unpredictable US president and hardliners in Tehran, were on Monday trying to coax both sides to put the conditions in place for a second round of talks i

stories.title: George Ariyoshi, first US governor of Asian American descent, dies aged 100
stories.summary: Democrat led Hawaii from 1973 to 1986, coinciding with the party’s rise to power in the state George R Ariyoshi – Hawaii’s former governor and the nation’s first Asian American governor – has died at age 100. Ariyoshi, a Democrat who led the state from 1973 to 1986, died peacefully while surrounded by family on Sunday night, according to a statement Monday from the current governor, Josh Green. Continue reading...

stories.title: Unpacking the 2026 Venice Biennale controversies and highlights
stories.summary: Russia's return to the international contemporary art exhibition, as well as the participation of Israel and the US, have sparked politicized backlash.

stories.title: UK: Starmer says he would't have appointed Mandelson as US envoy if he'd known he failed vetting process
stories.summary: Under-fire Prime Minister Keir Starmer told the House of Commons that neither he nor his ministers were told that the former ambassador to the US and Epstein associate Peter Mandelson had failed his security vetting.

stories.title: Tim Cook to step down as Apple CEO, John Ternus to take over
stories.summary: John Ternus, who has long focused ​on design and ‌products at Apple, will lead the iPhone maker as it gears up for an industry change spurred by artificial intelligence.

stories.title: Why cloud computing still runs on coal and gas
stories.summary: Renewable energy is increasingly being used to supply power-hungry data centers. As the sector swells, much of the electricity demand is being met by polluting fossil fuels.

stories.title: Apple switches CEO: What it means for the tech giant
stories.summary: Apple's top hardware engineer John Ternus will lead the $4 trillion iPhone maker, as Tim Cook steps back. Will Apple chase rivals for the smartest intelligence, or deliver the best personal AI experience?

stories.title: The insider trading suspicions looming over Trump's presidency
stories.summary: The BBC has found a pattern of spikes in trades ahead of public announcements by the US president.

stories.title: Netanyahu condemns IDF soldier for vandalising Jesus statue in southern Lebanon
stories.summary: Israel's Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu has condemned the desecration of a statue of Jesus Christ that was carried out by an IDF soldier in a southern Lebanese village.

stories.title: Victory slips away as marathon runner celebrates too soon
stories.summary: The dramatic end to the Delaware Marathon occurred when the lead runner slowed in celebration before a trailing marathoner sprinted toward the finish line .

stories.title: Turkey arrests 90 people with suspected links to so-called Islamic State group
stories.summary: Authorities have not officially connected the arrests to a shootout outside the Israeli consulate in Istanbul on 7 April in which two police officers were wounded.

stories.title: FBI director Kash Patel files $250m defamation suit against The Atlantic
stories.summary: According to The Atlantic, Patel's job as head of the Federal Bureau of Investigation is in jeopardy in part because of what it said were "bouts of excessive drinking" and "unexplained absences".

stories.title: Pope Leo XIV holds a giant open air Mass in Angola
stories.summary: Tens of thousands of people attended a giant open-air mass by Pope Leo XIV outside the Angolan capital.

stories.title: Trump refuses to lift Hormuz blockade until Iran deal agreed
stories.summary: Trump’s announcement comes after Iran said earlier it would not be sending a delegation to participate in the second round of high-stakes peace talks in Islamabad that were set to take place on Monday.

stories.title: Gunman opens fire on tourists at Teotihuacan pyramids in Mexico, killing one
stories.summary: Mexican authorities launched an investigation to uncover the motives behind the attack, which took place just before noon local time.

stories.title: Hot air balloon makes emergency landing in California backyard
stories.summary: "Look at that, wow!" - A couple in Temecula woke up to find a balloon boarded with 13 people behind their home on Saturday.

stories.title: US Labour Secretary Chavez-DeRemer quits amid misconduct probe
stories.summary: Us labour secretary Lori Chavez-DeRemer quits Trump cabinet amid misconduct probes, including alleged affair with a subordinate and misuse of staff.

stories.title: Mexico City: Video shows armed man shooting at tourists at Teotihuacán pyramids
stories.summary: Video and photos published by local media shows a man standing with a gun on top of a pyramid while people duck for cover. A number of gunshots ring out in the videos.

stories.title: Timelapse footage shows maritime traffic around Gulf, Straight of Hormuz
stories.summary: The passage of ships through the Strait of Hormuz - the world's busiest oil shipping canal - has been severely disrupted amid the US-Israeli war with Iran. Timelapse footage shows maritime traffic in the Gulf and Strait of Hormuz from April 18 to April 20, amid a fragile US-Iran truce.

stories.title: Iran hangs man accused of ties to Israel and setting mosque on fire during pre-war protests
stories.summary: Iran executes Amirali Mirjafari for allegedly plotting to burn a Tehran mosque and collaborating with Israel and the US, as rights groups decry rising executions.

stories.title: Japan loosens arms export rules in break from post-WW2 pacifism
stories.summary: This clears the way for Japan to sell weapons to more than a dozen countries.

stories.title: Madonna offers reward for return of missing Coachella costume
stories.summary: The items, which she says are "part of my history", disappeared after her slot with Sabrina Carpenter.

stories.title: Dozens of Turkish coal miners detained after march for unpaid wages
stories.summary: Some 110 Doruk Madencilik workers who had been marching to Ankara to demand unpaid wages and compensation were detained as demands for rights and safety grow louder.

stories.title: Gaza needs more than $71 billion for recovery and reconstruction, EU-UN report says
stories.summary: Gaza would need over $71 billion for recovery after two years of war, EU and UN warned Monday, as damage to homes, hospitals, and schools leaves most people displaced.

stories.title: How will Apple change under 'product guy' John Ternus?
stories.summary: How Ternus responds to challenges facing Apple will go a long way to deciding the tech giant's future, writes Zoe Kleinman.

stories.title: Germany News: Neighbor attacked for pounding schnitzel too loudly
stories.summary: Police in southern Germany were called in to settle a schnitzel dispute that had turned violent, with a man reportedly slapping his neighbor for pounding his schnitzel too loudly.

stories.title: WWII bomb destroyed in controlled explosion in Paris
stories.summary: Video provided by French police shows the controlled detonation of a large bomb dating from the Second World War in Colombes. After attempts to remove the bomb’s detonator failed at midday, authorities buried it and later destroyed it in a controlled detonation.

stories.title: LensCulture portrait awards 2026 – in pictures
stories.summary: Winning and shortlisted works move seamlessly between documentary and invention. Across these images, tender couples hold each other close, people reclaim their identities from the burden of colonial memories and the harsh realities of the war in Ukraine come sharply into focus Australian photographer wins at world photography awards with ‘barefoot volcanologist’ image Continue reading...

stories.title: Charlize Theron joins chorus of disapproval over Timothée Chalamet’s ballet comments
stories.summary: The former ballet dancer said Chalamet’s comments were ‘reckless’ in an interview with the New York Times in which she also discussed her violent childhood Actor and former ballet dancer Charlize Theron has joined the chorus of disapproval aimed at Timothée Chalamet over his remarks that appeared to disrespect performers of ballet and opera . In an interview with the New York Times , Theron said: “Oh, boy, I hope I run into him one day,” adding: “That was a very reckless comment on two art forms
